command: [Net.ServicePointManager]::SecurityProtocol = [Net.SecurityProtocolType]::Tls12; $ErrorActionPreference='Stop'; $domain='example.com'; $out='C:\ProgramData\recon\securityscorecard_example.com.json'; New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path C:\ProgramData\recon | Out-Null; $log='C:\ProgramData\recon\recon.log'; try { $token=$env:SSC_API_TOKEN; if([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($token)){ throw 'SSC_API_TOKEN not set'; } $headers=@{ 'Authorization'="Token $token"; 'Accept'='application/json' }; $uri="https://api.securityscorecard.io/companies/$domain"; $resp=Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $uri -Headers $headers -Method Get -UseBasicParsing -TimeoutSec 45 -ErrorAction Stop; if(($resp.StatusCode -ge 200) -and ($resp.StatusCode -lt 300) -and ($resp.Headers['Content-Type'] -match 'application/json')){ ($resp.Content | ConvertFrom-Json) | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 6 | Set-Content -Path $out -Encoding UTF8 } else { Add-Content -Path $log -Value "$(Get-Date -Format o) SecurityScorecard non-success or non-JSON for $domain. Status=$($resp.StatusCode)" } } catch { Add-Content -Path $log -Value "$(Get-Date -Format o) SecurityScorecard error for $domain: $($_.Exception.Message)" }
explanation: Retrieves organization security ratings from the paid SecurityScorecard API (closed-source) using token authentication and stores normalized JSON.
rationale_steps: Authenticate with a token to a subscription service.
Request the company profile by domain using HTTPS.
Validate and convert JSON output for storage.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Well-formed PowerShell with balanced quotes/braces, valid hashtable syntax, proper separators, and correct cmdlet/parameter usage.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It authenticates to and queries a paid SecurityScorecard API for organization data and stores the JSON, matching T1597’s use of closed-source intelligence sources.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell command is well-formed with balanced braces, parentheses, and quotes, proper semicolons as separators, valid cmdlet syntax, and correct use of try/catch error handling throughout.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements T1597 by querying the paid SecurityScorecard API (a closed commercial intelligence source) using token-based authentication to retrieve organizational security ratings for a target domain, storing results for reconnaissance purposes, which aligns precisely with the technique's description of querying paid technical databases to collect targeting intelligence.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell script is well-structured, uses correct syntax for variables, try-catch blocks, and cmdlet parameters, with all brackets and quotes properly balanced.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements the T1597 technique by querying a non-public, paid security intelligence API (SecurityScorecard) to gather reconnaissance data on a target domain.
